hypothesis: If CHROMA is scaled to larger encoder sizes (1B, 3B, 8B) and deeper decoders (2, 4, 8 layers), then the resulting designs will exhibit lower CIE76 ΔE color differences to the target colors across training-paraphrase and validation prompts and multiple viewing angles, compared with smaller configurations, because increased model capacity better captures the mapping from natural language to discrete material-thickness choices.
